calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Continued To Feb-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Special Administrator.

The Court notes that we now have a special administrator and once again raises the question of how the issue of conflict is resolved with having the Mother's Mother appointed. Ms. Friedeberg stated that they had a hearing in Family Court and that Judge Begert signed an order setting aside the judgment on the marital agreement so it is as though the Decedent died while being married. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that they need to make sure there is no separate property and that the Family Law Attorney seems to think that the only separate assets are earnings after the date of separation. In addition, Ms. Friedeberg stated that if there are no assets, this goes away and the guardianship goes away. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to February 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court signed an order extending the special letters to February 19, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Continued To Jan-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for and with Esther Wojcicki.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to January 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard after the guardianship matter is addressed. The Court raised the issue of providing sufficient time for notice. Ms. Friedeberg will provide an order extending the special letters to January 22, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Continued To Dec-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia for Trisha Friedeberg on behalf of Esther Wojcicki.

The Court raised the issue of the original petitioner (Janet Wojcicki) having withdrawn her petition. Ms. Navia stated that the assumption is that the petition is withdrawn and that it can go off calendar. The Court questions timing and whether the appointment of Esther should await her actual appointment as Guardian. Ms. Navia stated that Ms. Friedeberg is working with her Client to work out a date to have both petitions heard at the same time, requested a continuance to December 18th to have publication done and for getting a date for the guardianship, stated that this is at the forefront of discussion and that Ms. Friedeberg expects the special letters to come up in the guardianship matter on December 4th or December 11th. The Court informed Ms. Navia that the matters can not be heard at the same time as they are being heard in two different calendar types, different times and different dates. The Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Janet is off calendar. The Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Esther is continued to December 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court asked that Ms. Friedeberg give some thought to the issue of if a conflict arises from the Mothers efforts to undo the divorce, is the conflict resolved by having the Mother's Mother appointed and does it make sense to waive bond or not. Special administration is extended to December 18, 2018 (order signed in open Court).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Nov-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Nov-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Nov-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Continued To Nov-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for (petitioner) Janet Wojcick.

The Court stated that a competing petition has been filed, that it has been set on a Monday calendar but will continue the matters to a Tuesday, and asked what will happen with the special administration. Ms. Friedeberg reported that there is $3,000,000.00 in accounts which needs to be continually monitored or there could be tremendous loss and that they have a hearing set for the special administration tomorrow. The Petition for Letters of Administration (along with the competing petition) was continued to November 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge:Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Ex Parte Petition To Expand Powers Of Special Administrator
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-09-2018 Continued To Oct-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for and with Janet Wojcick (Special Administrator).

Ms. Friedeberg stated that shortly before the Decedent died there was divorce decree with a number of problems, that her Client is in the process of petitioning to modify it or revoke the decree, and that this will determine if there is anything in the estate. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that her Client advised her that this may be a conflict and proposed having someone else/the Grandmother in place as the special administrator, and that the Family Law Attorney said it could take about 90-days. The Court stated that Ms. Friedeberg's Client has no priority and that it is not sure if the Grandmother does. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she believes the nominator has priority. The Court questions whether the Public Administrator was noticed and stated that it does not believe the Client's nominator has priority. The Second Amended Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court advised Counsel that special administration will not be extended again especially with the conflict issue and stated that it wants to see the statements to make sure the funds are where they belong. Ms. Friedeberg is to provide a proposed order extending special administration of the estate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Continued To Oct-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Janet Wojcick (Special Administrator).

Ms. Friedeberg stated that she is requesting a continuance on the Petition for Letters of Administration, that she submitted the request for publication, but it was not published on time, and that she is here to have the special letters extended. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The special letters are also extended to that date (order for the same signed in open Court).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Continued To Sep-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
